name: Pineapple Snow & Custard Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the gelatin, sugar, and salt in a saucepan.
2. Add water and place over low heat, stirring constantly until the gelatin is dissolved.
3. Remove from heat and stir in the pineapple juice. Chill until the mixture begins to thicken.
4. Stir in egg whites and beat with an electric mixer until the mixture holds its shape.
5. Spoon into dessert dishes and chill until firm. Serve plain or with soft custard sauce.
Soft Custard Sauce:
1. Combine all sauce ingredients except vanilla in a saucepan.
2. Cook over hot, not boiling water in a double boiler, stirring constantly.
3. When the custard coats a silver spoon, remove from heat and cool immediately by placing the pan in an ice-water bath.
4. Stir in vanilla before serving over fruit or gelatin.
Yield: Pineapple Snow - 6 cups (1/2 cup serving: 45 calories, 0.5 fruit exchange, 1.7 gm protein, 0 fat, 9.5 gm carbohydrate, 45.9 mg sodium, 96.4 mg potassium)
Soft Custard Sauce - 2 cups (1/4 cup serving: 47 calories, 0.5 skim milk exchange, 3.1 gm protein, 1.5 gm fat, 5.4 gm carbohydrate, 107.5 mg sodium, 92.4 mg potassium)
